title: Tesla Manufacturing Systems & Automation
subtitle: How Tesla actually builds cars — gigacasting, the factory-as-machine, and the manufacturing ideas reshaping industry
description: A practical course on Tesla's manufacturing approach built from primary sources: Tesla's investor day materials, AI day presentations, public engineering content, and direct product documentation. Covers the gigafactory model, gigacasting, the cell-to-pack approach, the factory-as-product philosophy, automation tradeoffs, the production-hell lessons, and the manufacturing principles that explain why Tesla is different from legacy automakers. Not pro-Tesla cheerleading; not anti-Tesla either. Just how it works.
audience: Engineers, operators, founders, and curious learners who want a real mental model of modern manufacturing through Tesla's specific lens. No automotive background required.
prerequisites: Curiosity about how physical things are built
No engineering or manufacturing background required
Basic comfort with reading numbers and process diagrams
outcomes: Explain Tesla's manufacturing philosophy and how it differs from legacy automakers
Describe the gigafactory model and what it means for cost / scale
Understand gigacasting — what it is, why it matters, and where it breaks
Reason about automation tradeoffs (where it works vs. the production-hell lessons)
Map the cell-to-pack and battery manufacturing approach
Apply Tesla-style first-principles manufacturing thinking to other industries
Avoid the Tesla-style mistakes (over-automation, vertical integration risks)

modules.title: Tesla's Manufacturing Philosophy
modules.summary: The first-principles approach: factory-as-product, vertical integration, software-first ops, and how it differs from legacy auto.

lessons.title: Why Tesla manufactures differently — the first-principles approach
lessons.objective: Map Tesla's manufacturing philosophy against legacy automotive thinking.

lessons.title: Factory as product — the machine that builds the machine
lessons.objective: Understand the factory-design-as-engineering-discipline approach.

lessons.title: Vertical integration — what Tesla owns vs. supplies
lessons.objective: Track the make-vs-buy decisions across Tesla's stack.

lessons.title: Software-defined manufacturing
lessons.objective: See how software and data shape the production floor.

modules.title: The Gigafactory Model
modules.summary: How a gigafactory works, what 'giga' actually means at this scale, and the economics that follow.

lessons.title: What a Gigafactory actually is
lessons.objective: Define the gigafactory model and walk through one end-to-end.

lessons.title: Scale economics — why bigger factories beat lots of small ones
lessons.objective: Understand the cost curves that justify gigafactory investments.

lessons.title: Supply chain on-site — co-locating suppliers
lessons.objective: Map Tesla's approach to bringing suppliers into the campus.

lessons.title: The energy + battery + vehicle stack at one site
lessons.objective: See how vertical stacking across products affects operations.

modules.title: Gigacasting and Body Manufacturing
modules.summary: The gigacasting innovation: making body parts in one shot instead of welding hundreds. What works and what's hard.

lessons.title: Gigacasting — what it is and why it matters
lessons.objective: Understand the high-pressure die casting that replaces dozens of stamped parts.

lessons.title: The cost and complexity tradeoffs of gigacasting
lessons.objective: Compare the gigacasting vs. traditional stamping economics.

lessons.title: Structural batteries and the cell-to-pack approach
lessons.objective: Map the integration of battery into vehicle structure.

lessons.title: Where gigacasting breaks down
lessons.objective: Recognize the limits — repair, supply chain risk, design constraints.

modules.title: Automation, Robotics, and Production Hell
modules.summary: Tesla's automation journey — the over-automation mistake of the Model 3 launch, what they fixed, and how to think about automation tradeoffs.

lessons.title: The 'production hell' lesson — over-automation backfires
lessons.objective: Walk through the Model 3 production crisis and what it taught Tesla.

lessons.title: Where automation works vs. where humans win
lessons.objective: Apply the right-tool-for-the-job framework to factory operations.

lessons.title: Robotics, Optimus, and the future labor model
lessons.objective: Map Tesla's bet on humanoid robotics in manufacturing.

lessons.title: Throughput and continuous improvement
lessons.objective: Understand the production-rate-as-religion operational discipline.

modules.title: Lessons and the Future
modules.summary: Manufacturing lessons applicable beyond Tesla, the strategic risks of Tesla's approach, and what to expect next.

lessons.title: Applying Tesla-style manufacturing to other industries
lessons.objective: Translate Tesla's principles to non-automotive contexts.

lessons.title: The risks — vertical integration, automation, supply concentration
lessons.objective: Map the strategic risks of Tesla's manufacturing bets.

lessons.title: Global scaling — Berlin, Shanghai, and beyond
lessons.objective: Understand how Tesla replicates its model internationally.

lessons.title: Competitor response and the future of auto manufacturing
lessons.objective: See how legacy and new entrants are responding to Tesla's playbook.
